steps.title: Identify the core contradiction
steps.content: The passage contrasts disco's public image against its actual merit: widely “ridiculed” and seen as “superficial,” yet “far less superficial than many people believe.” The key signal is the parenthetical “(subjects hardly unique to disco, by the way)”—love songs that supposedly define disco as vapid actually appear across all genres.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content:
C says disco has been “unfairly dismissed for the inclusion of subject matter that is also found in other musical genres”—love songs cannot justly be the basis for dismissing a genre as superficial when that genre also contains substantive themes about community and identity.

A contradicts the text (scholars engaged, not ignored), B invents an evolution never described, and D misplaces a pre-existing tradition as disco-born.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
